perturbation.original_text: I will not leave confidential or sensitive data in view of others who do not have a legitimate business reason to view the data and I will only share confidential data with persons having authorized access .
perturbation.changed_text: I may leave confidential data in view of others and I may share confidential data with persons not having authorized access .
perturbation.explanation: The original states that the data cannot be shared with other individuals and not left in plain sight. The modified sentence now permits these actions creating an in-text contradiction. This directly contradicts the confidentiality obligations defined in section A and the consequences outlined in section C.3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential . Accordingly, every University of Nebraska employee, student, volunteer, contractor, business associate, or guest who either has or will be given access to printed or online confidential data is required to read and acknowledge his or her understanding of this agreement before being granted access .
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the user *may* leave confidential data in view of others and share it with unauthorized personnel. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which mandates that anyone with access to confidential data is *obligated* to keep it confidential. The change introduces a direct conflict in obligations, rendering the agreement inconsistent and potentially unenforceable. The explanation confirms this.

perturbation.original_text: I will not discuss confidential or sensitive information or data in public pl aces and will not remove confidential or sensitive data from my work site without University authorization .
perturbation.changed_text: I may discuss confidential information or data in public places and may remove confidential data from my work site without University authorization .
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts discussing confidential data in public and removing it from the work site. The modified version permits these actions, creating a contradiction with the non-disclosure agreement's intent. This is contradicting what is specified in section B.2
perturbation.contradicted_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly allows discussing and removing confidential data, which directly contradicts the contradicted text's requirement to maintain confidentiality. The original text prohibited these actions, aligning with the confidentiality requirement, but the changed text reverses this, creating a direct conflict. This is a strong contradiction, as following the changed text would violate the confidentiality obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: I accept responsibility for appropriate handling and management of information and data whether I come into contact with such information through my authorized access or in error.
perturbation.changed_text: I am not responsible for appropriate handling and management of information and data whether I come into contact with such information through my authorized access or in error.
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes responsibility for handling data, while the change removes this responsibility. This directly undermines the entire agreement's purpose of ensuring data protection and creates a direct contradiction with the acknowledgement in section C.2 where the subject agreeded to these responsibilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I agree with these stated responsibilities.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text states 'I accept responsibility', which is in line with the contradicted text 'I agree with these stated responsibilities'. The changed text states 'I am not responsible', which is a direct contradiction to the contradicted text stating that the subject agrees to those responsibilities.

perturbation.original_text: I will maintain the conﬁdent iality of this information and if uncertain about the appropriateness of who this information may be shared with in the course of my authorized access , I will discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.changed_text: I have the option to maintain the confidentiality of this information and I do not have to discuss any questions I have with my supervisor or the Central Administration security o ﬃce. I understand that even the most innocuous ﬁle can contain conﬁdential information.
perturbation.explanation: The original text obligates the individual to maintain confidentiality and to consult their supervisor/security office if unsure about sharing information. The modified version changes this to an option ('have the option to maintain') and removes the obligation to consult ('do not have to discuss'). This creates an in-text contradiction by undermining the non-disclosure agreement's core purpose. This change directly conflicts with the statement in section A which states every person 'is obligated' to keep such data confidential
perturbation.contradicted_text: Every person who has been given access to a University of Nebraska information system or who has access to or knowledge of confidential or sensitive information or data, is obligated to keep such data confidential . Accordingly, every University of Nebraska employee, student, volunteer, contractor, business associate, or guest who either has or will be given access to printed or online confidential data is required to read and acknowledge his or her understanding of this agreement before being granted access .
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text by making confidentiality optional and removing the obligation to consult supervisors, directly contradicting the contradicted text, which states that everyone with access to confidential data 'is obligated' to keep it confidential. The change undermines the mandatory nature of the non-disclosure agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
